extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu attacked solo after VAN AERT Wout suffered a flat tire in the front group, winning by ~30 seconds with PHILIPSEN Jasper taking second in the velodrome sprint.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL Mathieu capitalized on VAN AERT Wout's flat tire during front group selection, exposing reliance on mechanicals over pure attacks.
PHILIPSEN Jasper demonstrated trackie skills by switching his sprint line inside the velodrome, beating VAN AERT for second place unexpectedly.
Early crashes at Sagan/DILLIER/MEEUS eliminated strong finishers before cobbles began, revealing pre-sector positioning fragility despite calm conditions.
Race speed approached 46 km/h record pace, suggesting aggressive early cobble selections rather than traditional late-race attrition favored strongest teams.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: Solo win after opponent mechanical shows ability to press advantage when clear, already second Monument of season
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Mechanical flat tire in front group cost likely victory, finished fourth despite having legs to contest
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Second place represents breakthrough one-day result, first top-10 classic finish ever despite being 10-time stage winner
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Crashed inexplicably early before cobbles began, second consecutive major crash after Tour of Flanders week prior
rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: negative: Crashed early in same incident as Sagan, eliminated strong finisher before cobbles
rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: negative: Went down in early crash, removed before cobbles despite being discussed as pre-race contender
rider_signals.GEE Derek: neutral: Made early three-man breakaway after two hours of attempts, showing selection timing matters
